identity.short_name: Two tangent lights illuminating a one-holed disk; compute 6P then set \pi\to1

subdomain(s): planar visibility in a vertical prism

problem_spec.summary: A vertical prism has a floor equal to a disk with a single congruent circular hole removed. Two lights sit above points where the hole circle meets the outer circle; illumination reduces to planar segment containment. Compute the illumination probability and a scaled \pi\to1 evaluation.
givens: The floor region is R = { (x,y) : x^2+y^2 \leq 72 and (x-12)^2+y^2 \geq 72 }.
The room is the vertical prism over R (vertical walls over ∂R).
Lights are at ceiling points whose planar projections are L1=(6,6) and L2=(6,-6); let L={L1,L2}.
A floor point Q is chosen uniformly from R (by area).
Q is illuminated iff the planar segment from Q to at least one Li lies in R.
Define E = 6P; then replace \pi by 1 in E and evaluate.
ask: Output the resulting integer as a zero-padded three-digit string.

parameter(s).name: hole_center
parameter(s).purpose: center of the removed congruent disk (here (12,0))

parameter(s).name: hole_radius_sq
parameter(s).purpose: removed disk has radius^2 = hole_radius_sq (here equal to outer_radius_sq)

parameter(s).name: light_points
parameter(s).purpose: planar projections of ceiling lights (here two intersection points)

parameter(s).name: scalar_postprocess
parameter(s).purpose: maps P to required output (here k=6)

invariant(s).name: prism_visibility_reduction
invariant(s).statement: In a vertical prism over R, a light ray stays inside the room iff its planar projection segment is contained in R.

invariant(s).name: convex_outer_disk_simplification
invariant(s).statement: Since the outer boundary is a convex disk, any segment between two points in/on it stays inside; thus obstruction is only from the (removed) hole.

invariant(s).name: tangent_halfplane_rule
invariant(s).statement: If a light point lies on the hole-circle boundary, then the segment to Q avoids the hole iff Q lies in the exterior half-plane determined by the tangent line at that light point.

essential_assumption(s): Light travels in straight lines with no reflection.
Boundary cases have measure zero and do not affect probabilities.
Q is uniform with respect to planar area on R.
uniqueness_certificate(s): R and L uniquely determine the illuminated set I by segment containment. The resulting probability P=Area(I)/Area(R) is uniquely defined. Scaling by 6 and substituting \pi\to1 are deterministic, so the final three-digit output is unique.

technique(s).name: reduce_3d_visibility_to_planar_segment
technique(s).purpose: convert 3D ray containment to planar segment containment

technique(s).name: separate_outer_convex_constraint_from_hole_obstruction
technique(s).purpose: use convexity to isolate the only obstruction (the hole)

technique(s).name: tangent_halfplane_visibility_from_boundary_point
technique(s).purpose: characterize visibility via tangent half-planes at boundary lights

technique(s).name: compute_area_of_floor_lune_via_circle_overlap
technique(s).purpose: compute Area(R) as disk area minus overlap lens

technique(s).name: compute_shadow_area_as_sector_minus_overlap
technique(s).purpose: compute the non-illuminated area by sector arithmetic

technique(s).name: probability_as_area_ratio_then_postprocess
technique(s).purpose: obtain P, form E=6P, substitute \pi\to1, and format output

theorem(s).name: convexity_segment_containment
theorem(s).role: segments between points in a convex set remain in the set

theorem(s).name: circle_lens_overlap_formula
theorem(s).role: provides exact overlap area for two congruent intersecting disks

theorem(s).name: tangent_supporting_halfplane
theorem(s).role: a tangent line supports a convex disk; exterior half-plane avoids the disk

theorem(s).name: geometric_probability_area_ratio
theorem(s).role: uniform point in region implies probability equals area ratio

library(s).name: shapely
library(s).reason: numerical area verification by polygonal approximation

task(s).name: numeric_area_sanity_check
task(s).reason: approximate Area(R) and Area(I) to confirm the closed form
